definition: Shopify's server-side templating language for rendering storefront HTML.
A merchant-editable theme module that can render configurable UI.
A repeatable content unit nested inside a section.
A reusable partial template, usually rendered with the render tag.
A template that declares section order and configuration for a page type.
Typed custom data attached to a Shopify resource.
A reusable structured content entry with typed fields.
A library for embedded app UX integration inside Shopify admin.
An app-managed storefront extension surface such as an app block or embed.
A backend extensibility unit for discounts, shipping, validation, and more.
The app-facing API for store data and operations.
The storefront-facing GraphQL API used in headless builds and custom buying experiences.
An event push from Shopify to your app when store data changes.
A cryptographic signature used to verify webhook authenticity.
Theme-side endpoints for cart and section interactions without full reloads.
Shopify's React framework for headless storefront development.
Shopify-managed hosting/deployment platform for Hydrogen storefronts.
Shopify's admin-focused design system and component library.
A GraphQL query explorer used to inspect schemas and test operations.
A linting tool for Shopify themes that catches common quality issues.
